paragraph: The single-family market in Fort Lee is sparse, with only 20 sales over six months at a median of $1,112,500. Homes are selling below list price, with a sale-to-list ratio of 0.98 and a median of 58 days on market. Sellers are okay with waiting for the right buyer rather than pricing aggressively to move fast. Multi-family properties are even thinner on the ground at 7 sales with a median of $1,365,000, showing similar patience from sellers. By contrast, condos and townhouses are the real volume of the Fort Lee market with 134 sales at a median of $620,000. This category includes new-construction side-by-side duplexes built on torn-down single-family lots, not only high-rise units. Buildings like the Buckingham, The Palisades, and Mediterranean South are moving units consistently, and the median days on market of 35.5 reflects steady absorption. Co-ops sit at the entry point with 98 sales at a median of $250,000. Horizon House, The Plaza, and Mediterranean Towers North and West are buildings to know, and they come with board approval, financing restrictions, and resale friction that not every buyer is aware of going in. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
